title: amerina glandulosa
intro: Amerina glandulosa, also known as American Goldenseal, is a plant species native to North America, characterized by its yellow roots and blue-green leaves. The medicinal properties of Amerina glandulosa root have been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly for its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of American Goldenseal include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and pneumonia, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. Amerina glandulosa is often prepared as a tincture or dried root extract, and can be consumed as a supplement or used topically in creams and ointments.
